name: Unusual process access to LSASS
description: Finds high-access Sysmon process-access events targeting LSASS from a non-system or non-Defender path. Legitimate security tools can still match and must be baselined.

attack.name: OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
attack.mapping_condition: Apply only when the process access is consistent with credential extraction from LSASS memory.

query.text:
index=<configured-index> sourcetype=XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational EventCode=10 TargetImage="*\\lsass.exe" GrantedAccess IN (0x1010,0x1fffff)
| where NOT like(lower(SourceImage), "%\\windows\\system32\\%") AND NOT like(lower(SourceImage), "%\\program files\\microsoft defender\\%")
| table _time host User SourceImage TargetImage GrantedAccess CallTrace SourceProcessId
| sort -_time

false_positives: Endpoint security, debugging, or approved forensic tooling
Organization-specific credential providers or authentication agents
triage: Validate the source binary path, signer, hash, user, and process ancestry.
Review nearby authentication anomalies and suspicious process or file activity.
If compromise is supported, preserve evidence and rotate potentially exposed credentials.
